command.placeholder: Jump to a character, spell, item, or rule…

data.title: Data folder
data.desc: Charnik keeps your characters and content here as plain files you can open, edit, and back up.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.desc: Community plugins extend effects with sandboxed code, read from the plugins/ folder inside your data folder. Every plugin is off until you review and enable it.
plugins.webOnly: Plugins run in the desktop app only.
plugins.none: No plugins found. Put a plugin folder under plugins/ in your data folder and refresh.

consent.title: Enable this plugin?
consent.sandbox: Runs sandboxed: no files, no network, no app access. It can only compute numbers for your sheet.
consent.name: Name
consent.author: Author
consent.warning: Only enable plugins from authors you trust.

roster.empty: No characters yet. Create one to get started.

feedback.title: Report a bug or leave feedback on GitHub

demo.title: Heads up — this demo only ships the free SRD content
demo.body: Charnik is in active development. We can only include the SRD — the part Wizards of the Coast released for free (CC-BY). The rest of D&D belongs to them, not us, so a lot of it legally can't be here out of the box: Artificer, Aasimar, the Beholder, most subclasses, and loads of spells & monsters. But nothing holds you back — make your own homebrew and, with the flexible CSV system, add whatever you want: from species and classes to spells. You can also download the full desktop app for your computer — it keeps all your data local and needs no internet connection.
demo.readonly: Editing isn't available in the demo. Download the desktop app to save changes.

contentMeta.title: Content metadata check
contentMeta.subtitle: Some content files are missing a bit of metadata — nothing's broken, this is a normal step. Fill it in below, or discard this dialog.

licenses.CC-BY-4.0: Others may use and adapt it, even commercially, as long as they credit you.
licenses.CC0-1.0: Public domain — anyone can do anything, no attribution required.
licenses.CC-BY-SA-4.0: Same as CC-BY, but adaptations must keep this same license.
licenses.MIT: Very permissive; reuse freely as long as the copyright notice stays.

contentDrift.title: Content data changed
contentDrift.subtitle: We noticed because a file's recorded hash no longer matches its contents — the data was edited outside the app. This is a normal step, we just want to confirm you meant it this way. Set today's date as the revision date and recompute the hash?

firstRun.title: Choose where Charnik keeps your data
firstRun.subtitle: Your characters and the SRD content live here as plain files you can open, edit, and back up any time. You can change this later in Settings.
firstRun.hint: A folder named “charnik” will be created here.

update.tooltip: Version {version} is ready — click to install and restart

homebrewForm.idHint: lowercase, dashes — e.g. fire-bolt. Leave blank to auto-generate from the name.
homebrewForm.editIdHint: The id is fixed while editing — your homebrew copy keeps it so it overrides the original.
homebrewForm.editForkNote: This entry ships with Charnik, so your edit is saved as a homebrew copy (the original stays untouched and your version sorts above it).
homebrewForm.editInPlaceNote: Editing your homebrew entry in place.
homebrewForm.srdWarnBody: Saving here works now, but a Charnik update can overwrite this file and your entry would be lost. Add it to your own file instead.
